title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum prices rose to 3626.75 USD/T on April 13, 2026, marking a 3.29% increase from the previous day, with a 6.95% rise over the past month and 52% year-over-year. UK aluminum futures reached around $3,550 per tonne, the highest since March 2022, driven by uncertainty from President Trump’s pledge to enforce a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Despite downside risks from weakening demand and surging energy prices, supply disruptions have caused aluminum to diverge positively.

title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Global aluminum prices held flat at $3,501.76 per ton, maintaining multi-year highs due to severe supply constraints. Iranian missile strikes damaged key smelters like Emirates Global Aluminium and Alba, while the Strait of Hormuz closure halted Gulf exports, causing LME inventories to plummet. Analysts forecast a 570,000-tonne global deficit, signaling significant upside risk.

title: Aluminum Shortages About To Begin As Supply Chain Wrecked
summary: Discusses challenges in aluminum production due to Middle East tensions, including Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA) declaring force majeure after Iranian missile and drone strikes damaged its Al Taweelah smelter. This has led to skyrocketing aluminum prices, with LME aluminum futures up 50% from a year ago. Supply chain disruptions are spreading to industrial metals beyond energy markets.

title: Aluminum Shortages Coming Soon | This Is Serious
summary: Highlights impending aluminum shortages from rising energy costs and global disruptions, including Iranian strikes reducing Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) to 30% capacity and shutting down Emirates Global Aluminium's Alawila smelter due to power plant damage. Warns that ongoing power issues, supply constraints, and security risks could force more facilities offline, removing substantial aluminum from the market amid high demand.

title: UAE and Bahrain's main aluminium producers say plants damaged by Iranian attacks
summary: Iran targeted major aluminum producers in UAE and Bahrain in the past 24 hours, damaging facilities of Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) and Aluminium Bahrain (Alba), with minor injuries reported. Alba is assessing damage while maintaining operations; EGA's Al Taweelah smelter, which produced 1.6 million tonnes in 2025, had stocks in transit. Related shutdowns include Alba's 19% capacity reduction due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions and force majeure declarations.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum prices rose to 3626.75 USD/T on April 13, 2026, up 3.29% daily and 52% yearly, driven by supply disruptions from Strait of Hormuz issues and Iranian strikes on regional smelters like Emirates Global Aluminium's Al Taweelah plant. Gulf region supplies 9% of global output but faces severe shipment halts; market shows backwardation on London Metal Exchange amid high prices near four-year highs.

title: Global Aluminium Can Recycling Reaches 75%, Marking Major Step Toward Circular Economy
summary: A global study reveals that aluminium cans achieve the highest collection-for-recycling and recycling rates among beverage containers, reaching 75% recycling rates. The findings were presented at COP30 in Belém, Brazil, demonstrating aluminium's leadership in closed-loop recycling and circular economy solutions.

title: AFRY Report Reveals Carbon Capture Could Enable Last-Mile Aluminium Decarbonisation
summary: A new report from AFRY highlights how Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS) technology could serve as a key pillar in achieving full decarbonisation of aluminium smelting. The research identifies CCS as a critical solution for the final stages of aluminium production decarbonisation.

title: Novelis Aluminium Recycling Success
summary: Novelis has begun operations at its recycling centre in Ulsan, South Korea, advancing its circular aluminium production approach. This facility represents a significant step in sustainable aluminium recycling technology.

title: Rio Tinto Indian Smelter Plan
summary: Rio Tinto is considering constructing an aluminium smelter in India powered by wind and solar energy. This project represents a major commitment to renewable energy-powered aluminium production.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,082 CNY/T on April 14, 2026, up 0.06% from the previous day, with rebar futures climbing above CNY 3,080 per ton as Chinese steel mills resumed production. Sharp drops in raw material costs improved mill margins, supporting higher output. Global supply concerns persist after failed US-Iran peace talks, though prior reopening of the Strait of Hormuz eased some disruptions.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel fell to 1,083.10 USD/T on April 13, 2026, down 0.36% from the previous day, though it has risen 2.47% over the past month and 15.47% year-over-year. Prices recently increased to 1075.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024, amid ongoing market gains.

title: Nucor raises HR spot price to $1,045/ton
summary: Nucor increased its consumer spot price for hot-rolled coil to $1,045 per short ton, a $5/st rise from last week. This adjustment reflects ongoing market dynamics in the US steel sector. Raw steel production also rose to a four-year high for the fourth consecutive week, per AISI data.

title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
summary: FOB Asia slab prices rose $24/t since late February, and CFR Asean billet prices increased $30/t, with Iranian prices strengthening $26/t higher for April shipments. A ceasefire announcement led to plunging European gas and crude prices, potentially relieving production costs, though risks of Strait of Hormuz disruptions remain high. Some Asian and European producers raised long product prices due to energy and freight costs.

title: Turkey’s Icdas cuts its longs prices amid sluggish demand
summary: Turkey’s Icdas reduced longs prices due to sluggish demand. Chinese steel section prices eased amid billet weakness and high output. Local Indian CRC and HRC prices showed mixed trends despite mill hikes, reflecting divergent outlooks.

title: US raw steel production increases by 0.7 percent - week 15, 2026
summary: US raw steel production rose by 0.7% in week 15 of 2026. This aligns with recent data showing domestic production at 1,843,000 net tons for the week ending April 11, with a capability utilization rate of 79.8%. The increase reflects ongoing improvements in output and efficiency.

title: India steel carbon emission policy targets 25% reduction and 400 million mt capacity by 2036
summary: India's new steel policy aims for a 25% reduction in carbon emissions alongside expanding capacity to 400 million metric tons by 2036. This initiative addresses environmental goals while boosting production scale. It comes amid rising iron ore output and strong demand forecasts.

title: India's iron ore production rises 7% in FY'26 as merchant supply offsets fall in captive production
summary: India's iron ore production increased 7% in FY 2026, driven by higher merchant supply compensating for declines in captive production. This supports the steel sector's raw material needs amid capacity expansion plans. The growth underscores robust upstream activity for steelmaking.

title: Tensions in Strait of Hormuz drive up energy and logistics costs, raising risks for steel sector
summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz are escalating energy and logistics costs, posing risks to the steel sector. Higher costs could impact production margins and supply chains globally. Steel producers are monitoring the situation closely for potential disruptions.

title: Danieli to Deliver New ERW Tube Line to Utva Silosi AD Kovin
summary: Danieli announced it has been selected to supply a new electric-resistance welded (ERW) tube production line to Utva Silosi AD Kovin in Serbia. This innovation enhances tube manufacturing efficiency and precision in steel production.

title: Free India steel carbon emission policy targets 25% reduction and 400 million mt capacity by 2036
summary: India has introduced a steel carbon emission policy aiming for a 25% reduction in emissions alongside expanding capacity to 400 million metric tons by 2036. The policy promotes sustainable steel production through technological advancements and efficiency measures.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development is significant for copper production as El Teniente is a major copper operation. The restart could help stabilize supply amid current market pressures.

title: Ivanhoe Mines Provides 2025 Production Results, 2026 Production Guidance
summary: Kamoa-Kakula produced 388,838 tonnes of copper in concentrate in 2025, achieving guidance, with record production from the Phase 3 concentrator. The company confirms 2026 production guidance of 380,000 to 420,000 tonnes of copper. Copper anode production is averaging 500 tonnes per day, with first exports imminent.

title: Freeport Indonesia aims to produce 1.1B lb. of copper cathode in 2026
summary: Freeport Indonesia targets 1.1 billion pounds of copper cathode production in 2026. The company is ramping up operations following a fatal landslide that disrupted activities in the second half of the previous year.

title: Chile’s new government looks to speed up copper expansion amid tight supply
summary: Chile’s new government under President José Antonio Kast aims to accelerate copper expansion to reach 6 million metric tons annually in the next four or five years. This responds to tight global supply conditions.

title: Copper outlook strengthens as long-term assumptions climb
summary: Higher long-term copper price assumptions indicate a structural market shift driven by demand growth and supply constraints. This strengthens the copper market outlook.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto has produced the first copper cathode using its proprietary Nuton bioleaching technology at the Johnson Camp mine, relying on on-site microorganisms after over 30 years of R&D. The technology enables cleaner, faster, and more efficient production, targeting 30,000 tonnes of refined copper over four years with a mine-to-metal carbon footprint of 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper—the lowest in the U.S.—powered by 100% renewable energy certificates. This breakthrough demonstrates industrial-scale viability for sustainable copper production in just 18 months from concept.
